nodes.body: Addressed: stiffened the detection logic to verify extracted suffix using the 'matches' helper.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

You can use the `matches` helper function defined on line 52 here to keep the code consistent and readable, as it performs the exact same check.

```suggestion
    else if (matches(lastLine)) {
```
Addressed: using the 'matches' helper now for consistency.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

There is significant redundancy in this implementation. Both the `Main.vue` component and the underlying `aiProcess` (called via `fetchCorrection`) perform store lookups and call `resolveSlashCommand` on the same input. Consider refactoring the AI processing layer to accept the already resolved prompt data to avoid duplicate processing and store access, and to ensure consistency between the UI display and the actual AI request.
Addressed: refactored aiProcess to accept pre-resolved data, avoiding redundant store lookups and slash command resolution between the UI and AI layers.
